CalifOrnia
Fair Political
Practices Comrnission
March 29, 19 91
Malco1m A. Mackenzie
COOMBS & DUNLAP
Attorneys at Law
1211 Division Street
Napa, CA 94559-3398
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-9I-LO2
Dear I{r. Mackenzie:
This letter is in response to your request for advice
regarding the duties and responsibilities ot city of calistoga
councilnernber Robert Maxfield under the confl ictlof- inteiest
provisi-ons of the Political Reform Act (the ,asg"1.1 i""r-f.tt".
does not address a particular decision pending beiore yo..i "g"rr"y
but rather seeks general guidance. Accordingiy, we trlai your
letter as a req[uest for informal assistance under the provisions
of Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed) .2 please note that our
advice is linited to prospective decisions and we make no comments
regarding past conduct.
We do not provide advice regarding provisions of lae/ other
than the Act. We suggest that you consult with the office of the
Attorney ceneral regarding issues that nay arise under Section
1090 which prohibj-ts public offj.cials frorn contracting with their
agencies .
1 Go.r"rrrrant Code Sections 8lOOO-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commissi.on
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regnrlations Section
18000, et seq. All references to reg"ulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2 fnforroal assistance does not provide the requestor lrith the
irnrnunity provided by an opinion or fonnal written advice.
(Section 83114, Regulation 18329 (c) . )
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacranirento CA 958O4-O8O7 . (916) 322'5660


Flle No. ■-9■―■02
Page No。 2
OUESTTON
- 
Yay councilmember ltaxfield participate in governmental
decis ions - regarding the sale ot iis prolerty to-trre-diiy-oi
Cal istoga?
CONCLUS ION
councilroenber Maxfield nust disqualify hirnself and abstain
fron.participatins in any sovernnentil e;ai=i;;-;;;";;i";-Ihe 
sale
"I l.l= property to the clty or calistoga. Howeveri the irovisions
of the Act do not prohibit him fron apiearing reroie -irr"-.ity i"
the same manner as any other nember oi-the 96n;;;i-;dii"-;"
represent his personal interests.
FACTS
.. - The City of Calistoga wishes to acquire land for irrigation
with treated waste water. a lirnited nuriber ot par-ei s -oi-iucarrt
land within the city nay be available for these p".p;.";: 
one of
these parcels is thl property of Councilnemher }Iaxf ie1d.
. Late-1ast yeai,.a cornnittee was appointed, consisting of
members of the council and staff. 
ttr.- i{axfiel& aia "oi- '
participate in the decision to appoint thia-;;;iat""l-tir"
selection of its nenbers, or a dliinition of the role of this
committee. The conmittee has now roade its refort-i"-tir. .itv
council and has recommended that the city conii-". -irr"--""q"is ition
of Mr. I'Iaxf ield I s property .
You are the city attorney for the City of Calistoga. In this
capacity, you provide lega1 advice to the aity c;il;ii]-'vou 
trave
prepared a memorandun for the city council di-scussing tirese issues
and have subrnitted the menorandun for our consideratlon. in this
menorandum, you correctly point out that liIr. Uaxfiela nay not
participate as a public oriicia:. in any decision i"q"rdii"-'tn.
sare. of his parcel to the city. 
vou s3ek our aavic6 ["-d6t.rrine
whether there are reg'ulations, guicielines or other printed
rnaterial available to. gui.de a nunicipa).ity through [rr"- lo.=iur"
acquisition of property from a nembei ot i,ne citf councii-ina
lrhether commission staff has any connents or aaaitionii-Infut to
supplenent the rnaterial contained in your written n.rnoi.nai..
ANALYSTS
rnitialry we restate that the commission has no jurisdiction
over laws other than the Act. We suggest that you su6rnit this
inquiry to the Office of the ettornef-ceneral for additional
information and consideration.
We turn now to a discussion of the Act as it applies to your
facts. Section 87100 prohibits public officials frorn rnaking,
participatinq in, or using their official position to j.nfluence a
governnental decision in which they know or have reason to knov,
they have a financial interest. Menbers of the city council are
public officials. 
(Section 82048. )


File No. I-9■
―■02
Page No. 3
An official 
has a financial interest in a decision !,rithin the
meaning of Sectj-on 8710O if it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the pubLic generally, on the official, 
or on a
member of his innediate fanily, r or on:
(b) Any real property in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars (S1,0o0) or more.
Section 8 2103 (c) .
You have advised us that Councilmember lrIaxf ield owns real
property rrhich the city nay seek to purchase from the
counci lmember. For purposes of our analysis rre assume that Mr.
llaxf ie1d,s interest in this property is in excess of $1,000.
Consequently, Mr. Maxfield utust disqualify hinself from
participating as a public official 
in decisions regarding the sale
of his property to the city if it is reasonably foreseeable that
such decisions will have a material financial effect on ltr.
Maxfield,s financial- interests which is distinguishable from the
effect on the public generally.
Foreseeabi l itv
The effects of a decision are reasonably foreseeable if there
j-s a substantial- likelihood that they wiIl, occur. To be
foreseeable, the effects of a decision must be rnore than a mere
possibitity; 
however certainty is not required. 
(Do$rnev cares v.
Doi.rnev Communitv Development Comm. (L987) 196 CaI.App.3d 983, 989-
991; witt v. I'torro!, (L977) 70 Cal.App.3d 817; In re Thorner (1975)
1 FPPC Ops. 198 (copy encLosed). ) The Act seeks to prevent more
than actuaf conflicts of interest; it seeks to prevent even the
appearance of a possible conflict of interest. 
(Witt v. Morrow,
supra at 823. )
when a decision before the public off j.cial, involves real
property in which the official 
has an investrnent of $1,00o or
more, the effect of the decision is foreseeable. Disqualif ication
is required if the effect of the decision upon the public
official's 
interest in real property wil-1 be materj.al-.
Materia l itv
The effect of a decision is material, when an official's
economic interest is directly involved in the decision if:
(A) The decision involves the zoning or
rezoning, annexation or deannexation, saIe,
purchase, or l-ease, or inclusion in or exclusion
3  An officia■′s ':immediate fami■y:' are his spouse and dependent
children。  (Section 32029.)


FiIe No. I -9l,-102
Page No. 4
from any city, county, district or other local
governmental subdivision, of real property in which
the official 
has a direct or indirect interest
(other than a leasehold interest) of $1,ooo or
more, or a sinilar decision affecting such
property .
Regulation la702.1(a) (3) (A) (copy enclosed) .
Therefore, the decision to purchase the Maxfield property
would have a naterial financial effect on the land which is owned
by a city councilmember. Unless Ur. Maxfiel-d expects no
compensation from the city for the transfer of his land, he must
disqualify himself from participating in any decj-sion regarding
this transaction.
Public Generally
Even when the effect of a decision will be material , a public
official- nay participate in decisj,ons which will have an effect on
the public official 
e/hich is similar to the effect of the decision
on a significant segment of the public. 
(Regulation 18703, copy
enclosed. ) For the public Aenerall-y exception to app1y, the
decj-sj-on must affect the public official 
j-n a manner which is not
distinguishable fron the effect of the decision on the general
public. 
The general public consti,tutes all the resj-dents of the
jurisdiction of the agency, in this instance the city of Calistoga.
The effect of any decision regarding the purchase of Mr.
Maxfield's land will not have a sirnilar effect on residents of the
city. 
Thus, the public generally exception does not aPPIy. we
conclude that, under the provisions of the Act, Mr. Maxfiefd must
abstain from participating as a public official 
in any decision
regarding the sal-e of his land to the cj-ty.
Mr. MaxfieLd rnay, however, represent his interests before the
city in his private capacj.ty. Regulation 1870o.1-(b) (copy
enclosed) stites that an official, is not attempting to use his or
her official 
posi,tion to influence a governnental decision if the
officiaL:
(1) Appears in the same manner as any other
member of the general public before an agency in
the course of its Prescribed governmental function
so1ely to represent hinself or herself on a matter
which is related to his or her personal interests'
An official,s 
trpersonal interestsrr include, but are
not linited to:
(A) An interest in reaL property which is
who11y owned by the official 
or members of his or
her irnmediate family.
Regulation 18700. 1(b) .


File No. r-91-102
Page No. 5
Therefore, although Mr. uaxfield may not
participate in governmentaL decisions regarding the sale
of his property to the city in his capacity as a public
official, 
Mr. lIaxf ield may apPear before the city to
represent his personal interests.
we trust this letter adequateLy responds to your
inquiry. 
should you have any further questions regarding
this matter, do not hesitate to call ne at (916) 322-
5901.
S incereJ,y,
scott HaLlabrin
Acting ceneral counsel
